summary: iter-206 frontend test for Implant Database chips strip BLOCKED by stuck loading spinner on /admin/implant-catalog screen. Backend API confirmed correct (9 Alpha Bio systems with expected component counts). Frontend code review of iter-206 changes (auto-select gate at lines 177-200 + chips strip at lines 405-462) looks correct, but UI could not be visually verified because the catalog screen never finishes rendering: body.innerText is empty, only a spinner is shown, no console errors, no failed network requests, no Alert dialogs. Reproduced after 5+ retries (direct page.goto + in-app tile-menu navigation + reload).

ui_bugs.issue: Page stuck on loading spinner indefinitely. Body text remains empty for 30+ seconds across multiple retries (direct deep link, hamburger → tile menu, post-login navigation, page.reload). No console errors, no 4xx/5xx responses, no Alert dialog. Brand dropdown (data-testid='catalog-brand-dropdown') never appears, so the iter-206 chips strip cannot be verified end-to-end in the web preview.

action_items: Investigate why /admin/implant-catalog stays on loading=true forever in the web preview. The load() callback (lines 92-102) sets loading=false in finally{} so either (a) AuthContext's `user` never resolves on this route (the `if (!user) return;` guard at line 109 short-circuits load), (b) the api.get('/implant-catalog') promise hangs, or (c) the SafeAreaView ancestor renders a global ActivityIndicator before this component mounts. Likely candidate: AuthContext rehydration timing — please add a console.log in the useEffect at line 108 and check whether `user` is ever truthy on this route in web.
Recommend adding a fallback: if loading hangs >5s, surface a retry button so the screen is not silently dead.

Once the page renders, retest the iter-206 chips: 9 chips for Alpha Bio (SPI, NeO Conical Standard Connection, NeO Conical Hex Connection, NeO Internal Hex Connection, ICE, ATID, DFI, NICE, Surgical & Prosthetic Instrumentation), no auto-selected family on multi-system brands, single-system brands skip the chips strip, and component counts match (ICE/DFI/ATID=50, NICE=31).
critical_code_review_comments: Code review of iter-206 diff in /app/frontend/app/admin/implant-catalog.tsx looks correct — auto-select gate `if (familiesForBrand.length === 1)` properly guards the multi-system case (line 187), and the chips strip renders only when `familiesForBrand.length > 1` (line 426). data-testid attributes are present on every chip (line 449).

Cold-start hang on this screen has been a recurring symptom across iterations (referenced in iter-160's 'iter-160: Guard against firing the fetch before AuthContext has restored'). The guard may still be racing on web — consider an explicit `if (user === undefined) <Spinner/>` vs `if (user === null) redirect to login`.

success_rate.backend: 100% (API verified via curl - 9 Alpha Bio systems, correct counts)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend /api/implant-catalog returns {'systems': [...]} with 48 total records, 9 Alpha Bio (SPI=50, NeO Conical Standard Connection=36, NeO Conical Hex Connection=31, NeO Internal Hex Connection=50, ICE=50, ATID=50, DFI=50, NICE=31, Surgical & Prosthetic Instrumentation=0). Note: the request specified 8 Alpha Bio systems and 9 chips — the API has 9 records all under 'Alpha Bio' brand so chip count should be 9, not 8 as stated in the bug description. The /admin/implant-catalog screen is stuck on a loading spinner — the iter-206 UI changes could not be verified. To unblock UI testing, the main agent must fix the loading hang first. After login, navigate via hamburger (data-testid='hamburger-btn') → tile-menu-sheet → 'Implant Database' tile (it sits below viewport at 1920x1080 — scroll the modal sheet to reveal it).
rca of the issue: Cannot reproduce visually in browser because /admin/implant-catalog never escapes its initial loading state. Likely root cause: AuthContext's `user` is undefined/null when this screen mounts in the web preview, so the `if (!user) return;` guard at line 109 prevents load() from ever firing → setLoading(false) never runs → spinner forever. No network call to /api/implant-catalog was observed during the 30s wait, which corroborates this hypothesis. Mitigation: add a useEffect that, when `user` is undefined for >2s, redirects to /auth/login or shows a 'Session expired, please log in again' fallback.
